behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: manifest parser/resolver -> dependency graph -> vulnerability-check input set (MOCK-MISS). A unit test on one parser function can miss dropped transitive deps during wiring. No real OS/network/subprocess is required if vulnerability API is stubbed (INTEGRATION-BRAKE a=no). No AI output dependency (b=no) and no multi-agent chain (c/d=no). Rejected System because behavior text does not require real HTTP/OS interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner end-to-end in-process with fixture manifests (including nested/transitive deps) and a stub vulnerability client; assert every expected direct+transitive package/version is present in outbound check requests.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real vulnerability DB query semantics for version ranges (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). A stub can validate request shape but can hide real API interpretation differences (range syntax, normalization, inclusive/exclusive bounds). Not AI-dependent and not multi-agent. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model judgment is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vulnerability API sandbox/tenant, submit manifests with controlled version ranges and verify returned CVEs correspond to package+range matches, including edge bounds and non-matching ranges with same package name.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Single deterministic ordering rule on local data structure; no module boundary required to trigger failure (MOCK-MISS). No real infra, no AI, no multi-step pipeline. Rejected Integration because behavior text is pure sorting logic, not cross-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Call the sorter with mixed severities and CVSS ties; assert strict order Critical>High>Medium>Low and descending CVSS within each severity bucket.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is often parser/editor/serializer interaction: updating one dependency can reorder or mutate unrelated entries during write-back (MOCK-MISS). This needs multi-module in-process test with actual manifest files/serialization, but not real network/subprocess (INTEGRATION-BRAKE a=no). No AI dependency, no agent chain. Rejected System because behavior does not require external API/OS-specific primitives beyond local file handling.
behaviors.test_description: Run update flow on representative manifests; diff original vs output and assert exactly one dependency version token changed, all other keys/values remain byte-for-byte (or schema-equivalent if formatter is intentional).

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real repository PR creation and correctness of produced diff in actual VCS/API conditions (REAL-INFRA). Mocked Git/PR clients can hide auth, branch/base handling, API constraints, and server-side diff behavior (MOCK-HIDE). No AI output quality dependency and no multi-agent sequencing. Rejected Integration because behavior text says "real repository."
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner against a real test repo and real PR API credentials; verify one PR is opened, patch contains only the intended dependency bump, and no unrelated file changes are included.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is deterministic semver comparison logic in local code. No required external infra/module orchestration beyond function inputs, no AI dependency, no workflow chain. Rejected Integration because behavior text is algorithmic comparison, not inter-module state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Feed version pairs (including prerelease/build metadata edge cases) into breaking-change classifier; assert major bumps are flagged and minor/patch bumps are not.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration across modules: semver decision -> PR creator gate -> reporting output. A unit test of only the gate function can miss wiring regressions where PR call still fires or flag is not propagated (MOCK-MISS). No need for real network to assert skip behavior (INTEGRATION-BRAKE a=no); use spy/stub PR client. No AI dependency and no multi-agent sequence. Rejected System because the key failure is internal control-flow handoff, not external API behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner pipeline in-process with a breaking bump input and instrumented PR client; assert PR client is never invoked and scanner output contains review-required flag/reason.
